scope.summary: Extends the D7 AFTER LOGOFF rig to prove clean exit and SIGTERM logically close both the pinned Oracle session and the server-owned stateless pool session.
out_of_scope: changes to the sibling rust-oracledb driver repository

entry_points.name: scripts/rig/verify_logoff_lane.sh
entry_points.trace: Rig configures a unique Oracle MODULE per mode, enables a server-owned pool with min_idle=1, drives oracle_query to force the pinned session, requires at least two live v$session rows before shutdown, and asserts logoff row deltas and post-shutdown ghost counts.

entry_points.name: bash -n scripts/rig/verify_logoff_lane.sh
entry_points.trace: Passed shell syntax validation before commit ae137a9b1818c290a5e0c30ac21972c59a721207.

entry_points.name: git diff --check -- scripts/rig/verify_logoff_lane.sh tests/artifacts/evidence/b7b-logoff-verify-2026-07-22.txt
entry_points.trace: Passed whitespace check before commit ae137a9b1818c290a5e0c30ac21972c59a721207.

entry_points.name: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target cargo build -p oraclemcp
entry_points.trace: Built target/debug/oraclemcp from the current checkout before running the live rig.

entry_points.name: scripts/swarm_discipline.sh bounded-run --timeout 300 -- scripts/rig/verify_logoff_lane.sh
entry_points.trace: Passed: clean exit logged two rows and no ghosts, SIGKILL logged no rows, SIGTERM logged two rows and no ghosts.

limitations: The SIGKILL case remains a control only; B7b acceptance is clean exit and SIGTERM for pinned plus pooled sessions.
A4e remains skipped per operator instruction; no dependency was edited.
